response.justification: While Wei Chen demonstrates strong general software engineering skills and excellent communication abilities, this candidate fundamentally lacks the mandatory technical requirements. With only ~6 months of professional C++ experience (vs. 5-10 years required) and no Windows platform specialization, combined with a complete absence of capital markets, fixed income, or financial services domain expertise, the skill gap is insurmountable. The entire career trajectory is in web platforms, mobile, and backend development—entirely disconnected from the financial engineering domain required for this role.
strengths: Excellent communication and mentoring demonstrated through multiple workshops, talks, and open-source contributions
Strong CI/CD and DevOps infrastructure experience (GitLab, deployment, containerization)
Proven ability to master diverse technology stacks and architect systems across multiple domains
concerns: Critical gap: Only ~6 months of professional C++ experience (National Instruments, Dec 2014–June 2015) versus 5–10 years mandatory
No Windows platform experience; entire career focused on web, mobile, backend, and data platforms
Zero domain expertise in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services—a required competency

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (6 months vs. 5–10 years required)
Missing financial services/capital markets domain knowledge
Strong general software engineering and communication ability
